recommendation.title: Rest day — let TSB climb before Saturday's long run
recommendation.reasoning: Friday isn't a scheduled session this week — only Mon/Tue/Thu/Sat carry training in Week 1 — and that's the right call: TSB is -27.1, still past the -25 caution line from Jul 4's 22.75km untracked effort, even after two down days since. Recovery signals are mixed: HRV is 71ms, 6% above your 67ms baseline, and resting HR is a normal 40bpm, but sleep score is only 75 (from Apple Health, now the source of truth after the Zepp export's own sleep-score field turned out to be an unreliable local estimate) — decent, not great. This is mostly training-load fatigue (ATL 38.7 vs CTL 11.6) with sleep only partially helping the recovery case. Full rest lets TSB close some of that gap before Saturday's first long run.
recommendation.sessionDetail: No training scheduled. Full rest — no run, no strides. An optional short walk is fine if you want to move.
recommendation.bikeNote: No session to build the choice around tomorrow, but with TSB at -27 take the U-Bahn both ways for the work commute — save the legs rather than stacking the bike's aerobic load (this plan's own Week 1 note treats the 14km commute as 'extra zone 1') on top of an already deep fatigue hole, ahead of Saturday's long run.

sessions.lesson: Pace was noticeably slower than the week's other easy runs (5:69/km vs ~5:22) yet avg HR still hit 147bpm, above the ~142bpm easy ceiling — the accumulated fatigue (TSB around -27 to -32 all week) is making even a slower effort feel harder than it should.

Pace was right at the easy target (5:22/km) but avg HR of 153bpm is well above the ~114-142bpm easy zone — tempo-effort heart rate at an easy pace, consistent with that day's deep fatigue (TSB -31.7).
Swim class as scheduled, avg HR 112bpm — a normal aerobic pool effort, nothing to flag here.

The single biggest contributor to this week's fatigue — an 8.5-hour low-intensity effort (avg HR 89bpm); the cost was cumulative duration (2251kcal), not intensity, and it's still driving this week's deeply negative TSB five days later.

recentActivity.analysis: TSS this window is dominated by the Jul 4 outlier (186.9) and Jul 6's swim-plus-commute day (85.9); by Jul 8-9 load is back down to a normal easy-week level (14.9, 37.3), but TSB hasn't caught up yet (-27.1) — fatigue is lagging the return to normal training. Both tracked runs this week (Jul 7, Jul 9) show avg HR well above the easy zone at only moderate-to-slow pace, a repeating pattern that tracks directly with how high ATL still sits relative to CTL (38.7 vs 11.6).
